new_terms.context: ARM's codename for its first full datacenter CPU design, where ARM acts as a chip vendor. Meta is the first customer.
Describes ARM's new business model of designing and selling entire chips, not just licensing IP.
Data Processing Unit, mentioned alongside CPUs as a target for Neoverse core deployment in datacenters.
The majority owner of ARM, involved in the Stargate venture with OpenAI.
Cost of Goods Sold, a key financial metric mentioned in the context of ARM's new chip vendor business model.
Thermal Design Power, a key CPU specification. The Phoenix CPU has a configurable TDP of 250W to 350W.
A kit that enables coherent shared memory to attached XPUs over PCIe6 for the Phoenix CPU.
Codename for ARM's next-generation CPU design that will succeed Phoenix.
